For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking (39, 33) didn't change the board. The sky blue rectangle at (35, 32) is the slider's current position (the blue square in the lower structure). The submit button must be elsewhere.
Looking at the nodes:
- S (sky blue) at (12, 32) - top structure, 16 pixels (4x4)
- c (charcoal) at (28, 30) and (28, 34) - connectors
- S (sky blue) at (35, 32) - lower structure, 8 pixels (2x4 or 4x2)
The submit button in level 1 was at (60, 30). For level 2, maybe it's still at (60, 30) but I need to move the slider to a specific position first.
Wait, in level 1, the slider was horizontal and I moved it LEFT to the submit position. For level 2, the slider is vertical. Maybe I need to move it UP or DOWN to a specific position.
Let me look at the slider mechanism. The blue square is at (35, 32) currently. The slider track is vertical. Let me try moving the slider UP to the top position and then clicking (60, 30).
Actually, wait. In level 1, the submit button was at (60, 30) which is BELOW the 3x3 grid. For level 2, maybe the submit button is at a different location.
Let me look at the right edge green bar. In level 1, clicking the green bar reset the board. Maybe for level 2, clicking the green bar submits the solution.
But I already tried clicking the green bar earlier and it didn't reset. Let me try clicking it again now that the L-shape is correct.
Actually, let me reconsider. In level 1, the submit button was at (60, 30) which is the center of the bottom row of the 3x3 grid. But that's also where a tile is. So maybe the submit button IS one of the tiles, and I need to click it when the pattern is correct.
Wait, no. In level 1, (60, 30) was the submit button, and the 3x3 grid was at rows 50, 55, 60 with cols 25, 30, 35. So (60, 30) is the center-bottom tile of the 3x3 grid. But it was also the submit button.
Hmm, but in level 1, I clicked (60, 30) to submit, and it worked. For level 2, maybe I need to click a different position.
Let me look at the left panel. It has 3 yellow dots forming an L-shape. Maybe I need to click one of those dots to submit.
The yellow dots are at approximately:
- (51, 12) - top-left
- (51, 15) - top-right  
- (54, 15) - middle-right
Let me try clicking the bottom-right dot (54, 15) which is the "corner" of the L-shape.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking the slider didn't work. Try clicking the left panel (yellow dots area)
# The L-shape dots are at approximately (51,12), (51,15), (54,15)
# Try clicking the corner of the L-shape

result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 15}])
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

# If not, try the center of the left panel
if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    result2 = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 52, 'col': 13}])
    print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
    print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
